settings.editor:
Why It's Different

Backed by the Union, Not Just "Verified"

Three things that separate this from any informal agent you'd meet off the street.

settings.html:
✓

Dual Sign-Off Approval

Every agent is approved by both the SUG Director of Welfare and the Office of the President before they're allowed to operate.

🛡

ID & Business Verified

NIN or CAC documents, matric numbers, and office addresses are checked before any agent goes live.

⚑

Report & Accountability

Every agent can be reported directly to the Welfare office — and the Union, not just the agent, is accountable for the outcome.

settings.editor: This platform exists because students were dealing with housing agents nobody could vouch for. The SUG Welfare Directorate decided to fix that — not by writing a warning, but by building its own agency, with its own registered agents, working directly for the Union's interest.

settings.html:
"An agent on this platform isn't just someone we checked — they're someone the Union itself stands behind."
— SUG Director of Welfare

Sign-Offs Per Agent

settings.editor: An agent doesn't simply sign up. Each application goes through a structured process before it's allowed to go public.

settings.html:
• Full identity, business name, and contact details collected
  • NIN slip (individual) or CAC document (business) reviewed
  • Matric number and department confirmed for Student Agents
  • Reviewed first by the SUG Director of Welfare
  • Final joint sign-off from the Office of the President

settings.editor: Whether you want to apply as an agent or report an issue, the Welfare office responds within 24 hours.
